summary:
Nasogastric decompression has not been shown to prevent anastomatic leaks after laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy; current evidence lacks support for its effectiveness in this context. Thus, it remains unproven as a preventive measure despite being used routinely postoperatively. 

Final Answer: B

Only summarize the agent’s actual reasoning—do NOT add interpretation beyond what was stated.

Nasogastric decompression is not supported by evidence as a method to prevent anastomotic leaks after laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy. Current literature shows no link between nasal-gastric decompression and reduced leakage rates in this procedure.
